key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 cash from operations $9.566B minus capital spending $2.171B, divided by ~1,748M diluted shares (10-K FY2025)
Mid-single-digit revenue growth; Libre and cardiovascular mix sustain owner cash

open_questions: Confirm price source and base IRR after `marvin_cloud_refresh.py` run.
No approved third-party sources in base IRR.
thesis: Abbott Laboratories is a diversified global healthcare company selling branded generic drugs in emerging markets, infant and adult nutrition, laboratory and rapid diagnostics, and medical devices. **[Fact]** Customers are hospitals, labs, pharmacies, and consumers across more than 160 countries. **[Fact]** FY2025 net sales were **$44.3 billion** on four reportable segments: Established Pharmaceutical Products (**$5.5 billion**), Nutritional Products (**$8.5 billion**), Diagnostic Products (**$8.9 billion**), and Medical Devices (**$21.4 billion**). **[Fact]** Cash from operations was **$9.57 billion** and capital spending **$2.17 billion**, yielding about **$4.23** of free cash flow per diluted share. **[Inference]** Abbott is a wide-moat healthcare compounder whose economics are anchored in installed medical devices and recurring diagnostics/nutrition consumption, not one-time drug patents alone.
why_market_wrong: There is **no clear dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** The stock may embed fear of post-COVID diagnostics normalization and Q1 2026 margin compression while under-crediting durable device growth (continuous glucose monitoring, structural heart). Return at today's price is **normalized owner cash flow**, not a catalyst with a single payoff date. Peer comparison (Medtronic, Danaher diagnostics, Dexcom in CGM) frames whether Libre-led growth is already fully priced.
